commit.message:
Use lightweight regex PII scrubber on free tier to avoid OOM

Replaced the Presidio+spaCy PII scrubber with a dual-backend
architecture: "regex" (pure regex, zero deps, ~0 MB) and "presidio"
(full NER, ~250 MB).  PII_SCRUBBER_BACKEND env var controls which
backend to use; defaults to auto-detect.  Render free tier (512 MB)
now uses regex backend, freeing enough RAM for litellm + LLM calls.

Also removed `python -m spacy download` from Render build command
since the regex backend doesn't need it.

feat: add Overlay Protocol specification (v1.0.0-draft)

Formal spec for the non-invasive extension pattern unifying o-extension
(flag-based, adds new capabilities) and aggregate-library (dependency-based,
curates existing APIs) under shared invariants: non-modification, additive-only,
switchable, idempotent activation, and declared ECOSYSTEM.scm relationship.

Reference implementations: echidna/HOL-o-extension, developer-ecosystem/aggregate-library.

commit.message:
Add score fusion: keyword scoring, RRF hybrid search, and --min-score filter

Phase 3 of search improvements:
- keyword_score() ranks keyword results (title 2x, body 1x)
- reciprocal_rank_fusion() merges keyword + semantic rankings
- hybrid_search() method (feature-gated) applies structured filters
  before fusing keyword and semantic scores via RRF
- --min-score CLI flag filters results below a score threshold
- --semantic now triggers hybrid mode instead of semantic-only
- MCP lattice_search gains min_score param and hybrid support
- 10 new tests for keyword scoring, RRF, hybrid search, min_score

fix: open /dev/tty directly for TUI rendering

Bun has a bug where process.stderr is undefined when fd 2 is
redirected to /dev/tty (via shell integration's $() capture).
Open /dev/tty directly with openSync() to bypass this entirely.

Also handle process.stdout not being a TTY for getTerminalSize().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(kingdom-map): CoreLoreCascade — knowledge rings on agent searching state

When any agent enters `searching` state, CORE LORE emits expanding ring
waves radiating outward from the territory — hairline tori at emissive
intensity 2.2, expanding from radius 0.4 to 5.6 world units over 2.8s,
fading at the horizon.

Architecture: pre-allocated pool of 4 wave entries + mesh refs. Zero
allocation per frame. useFrame reads agentStates via getState() (no
subscription, no re-renders). Rotation [Pi/2,0,0] + scale.set(r,r,1)
gives uniform horizontal expansion on the XZ plane. depthWrite:false
prevents z-fighting when rings overlap. New wave every 1.8s while any
agent is in searching state.

This animation is unique to CORE LORE. No other territory does this.
Identity rings are static (they are the territory). These rings move
(they are the knowledge leaving the territory).

TypeScript: 0 errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix TypeScript type errors on tinaField calls for Cloudflare build

to tinaField() — the function requires an index signature that our
specific interfaces don't have. Uses a local tinaData cast to avoid
repeating the cast at every call site.

commit.message:
refactor(git): replace porcelain commands with plumbing alternatives (#1345)

Replace porcelain git commands with plumbing alternatives for
robustness:

- **`working_tree.rs`**: `git branch --show-current` and `git rev-parse
--abbrev-ref HEAD` → `git rev-parse --symbolic-full-name HEAD` with
`symbolic-ref` fallback for unborn branches. Structured output
(`refs/heads/<branch>` vs `HEAD`) instead of empty-string detection.
- **`diff.rs`**: `git show -s --format=` → `git log -1 --format=`
(single commit) and `git log --no-walk --format=` (multi-commit). `git
log` correctly resolves annotated tags to their underlying commit,
unlike `git show` which outputs tag metadata before the formatted commit
info.
- **`collect/mod.rs`**: Updated 3 stale comments referencing `git show`.

> _This was written by Claude Code on behalf of @max-sixty_

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix search performance: shared cache, fast inline verify, progress updates

Three root causes made search appear stuck with 0% CPU:

1. Cache mismatch: evaluator used .solver_cache.db but verify used
   .cache.db — every Z3 call was duplicated. Now both share one cache.

2. Inline verify checked sizes 2-8 with 30s timeout (up to 210s per
   child with global Z3 lock serializing everything). Now checks only
   sizes 2-4 with 3s timeout (max 9s). Full verification is done
   post-search via --check-models.

3. Progress callback only fired after entire beam completed. Now fires
   after each parent expansion. Also added text fallback every 5s.

Cold-cache benchmark: 319 evals in 44s at max-size 8 (was 61 in 857s).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
